metadata.generatedFrom: Itotori hello-world monorepo state after commit aa9339c
metadata.currentBaseline: DB-backed fixture hello world across Itotori, Kaifuu, and Utsushi

priorityDefinitions.P0: Critical orchestration or MVP blocker. A failed P0 audit blocks merge and must be repaired immediately.
priorityDefinitions.P1: Required for the MVP target. P1 audit findings block merge for the owning spec.
priorityDefinitions.P2: Important quality, scale, or post-MVP work. P2 findings may become new DAG nodes.
priorityDefinitions.P3: Exploratory, nice-to-have, or research work. P3 findings should be batched unless cheap.

statusDefinitions.complete: Already implemented and verified in main.
statusDefinitions.planned: Not started. It becomes ready when all dependencies are complete.
statusDefinitions.in_progress: Owned by an active branch or worktree.
statusDefinitions.blocked: Cannot proceed without a named decision or external input.

nodes.title: DB-backed monorepo hello world
nodes.summary: Current committed baseline: fixture extraction, deterministic draft, patch, delta package, runtime smoke report, DB-backed dashboard status, local and GitHub CI.
acceptanceCriteria: GitHub CI is green
GitHub Hello World workflow is green
Local just hello prints status=hello_world_passed
auditFocus: Baseline must not claim real engine support

nodes.title: MVP definition of done and release gate matrix
nodes.summary: Define exact MVP engines, fixture requirements, dashboard flows, quality/cost reports, non-goals, release checks, and demo script before integration milestones claim MVP completion.
acceptanceCriteria: MVP engines and fixture requirements are explicit
Required dashboard workflows and quality/cost reports are named
Known non-goals are documented

nodes.title: Runtime probe environment and fidelity policy
nodes.summary: Define static-vs-runtime evidence tiers, screenshot capability matrix, OS/browser/Wine assumptions, artifact limits, and wording that prevents static analysis pretending to be runtime.
acceptanceCriteria: Evidence tier is visible everywhere evidence is shown
OS/native/Wine/browser assumptions are explicit
Static analysis, runtime trace, screenshot capture, and VM playback are distinct

nodes.title: Product workflow and HITL UX spec
nodes.summary: Specify the human interaction points, decision queue information architecture, style-guide conversation, batch review model, and escalation policy before building UX-heavy flows.
acceptanceCriteria: Random line without context is explicitly rejected
Reviewers see source, draft, context, screenshots/evidence, and reasoning together
Human escalation is reserved for style disputes and high-value ambiguity

nodes.title: Engine fixture sourcing and reference implementation policy
nodes.summary: Define how engine fixtures and reference implementations are sourced, licensed, documented, and used by parallel adapter agents.
acceptanceCriteria: Every engine adapter has documented fixture source and support boundary
Reference implementation licenses are reviewed before porting
Unsupported encrypted or compiled variants fail with semantic capability errors

nodes.title: Localization surface inventory and contract ADR
nodes.summary: Define the canonical inventory of game localization surfaces and the contract source of truth before expanding schemas.
acceptanceCriteria: Dialogue, narration, speakers, choices, UI, tutorials, bestiary/database text, song titles, images-with-text, metadata, control markup, variables, ruby/furigana, and romanized/non-translated cases are covered
TS schema, JSON Schema, Rust serde, and generated binding responsibilities are decided
The inventory is engine-agnostic

nodes.title: Machine-readable spec DAG and orchestration docs
nodes.summary: Introduce the canonical roadmap DAG artifact, validation command, ready-node popper, and docs for the plan/implement/audit/merge lifecycle.
acceptanceCriteria: DAG validates structurally
Ready nodes can be listed deterministically
Docs define P0/P1 audit gating and P2/P3 reinsertion
auditFocus: Initial ready set enables parallel work

nodes.title: GitHub issue sync for DAG nodes
nodes.summary: Add a deterministic command that creates or updates GitHub issues from DAG nodes, preserving labels, dependencies, status, and acceptance criteria.
acceptanceCriteria: Dry-run output is stable
Existing issues are updated rather than duplicated
Every synced issue links back to its DAG node id
Issue sync is non-mutating by default; GitHub writes require an explicit apply flag

verification.value: Unit tests for issue body rendering

auditFocus: Issue text remains useful to implementation agents
Accidental issue mutation from a default command

nodes.title: Agent worktree lifecycle playbook
nodes.summary: Define and script the branch/worktree lifecycle used by the orchestrator: claim node, plan, implement, audit, repair, merge, and mark complete.
acceptanceCriteria: A coding agent can follow the lifecycle without hidden context
P0/P1 repair loops are explicitly documented
P2/P3 findings have DAG insertion rules
auditFocus: No process gaps around failed audits

nodes.title: Affected task detection and CI cache hardening
nodes.summary: Make CI fast and trustworthy for parallel work by formalizing affected-package detection, Vite+ cache usage, Rust cache usage, and required gates per project.
acceptanceCriteria: A docs-only PR skips expensive runtime checks safely
A schema change fans out to all dependent checks
CI still has one full-suite path before merge

verification.value: GitHub Actions dry run through a PR

auditFocus: CI results matching local just recipes

nodes.title: Golden fixture and private corpus policy
nodes.summary: Define how public fixtures, private purchased-game fixtures, hashes, metrics, and reproducible benchmark summaries are organized without redistributing copyrighted content.
acceptanceCriteria: Public CI can run with only open fixtures
Private local corpora can run without changing committed paths
Benchmark reports can cite hashes and aggregate stats
auditFocus: No copyrighted raw assets in repo

nodes.title: Spec audit templates and finding schema
nodes.summary: Standardize audit output so review agents can report P0-P3 findings that are machine-ingestible back into the DAG.
acceptanceCriteria: Audit output is parseable JSON plus human summary
P0/P1 findings block completion
P2/P3 findings can be converted into new node drafts

nodes.title: Toolchain version and upgrade policy
nodes.summary: Document and enforce how latest Node, pnpm, Vite+, TypeScript, Rust, dependency upgrades, lockfiles, and CI upgrade checks are managed.
acceptanceCriteria: Latest-and-greatest policy is explicit
just upgrade remains the canonical update command
CI failures after upgrades have a documented repair path

nodes.title: BDD and fixture-driven testing standard
nodes.summary: Define behavior-first testing conventions for TypeScript, Rust, DB, MSW, golden fixtures, property tests, mutation tests, and no-live-API CI.
acceptanceCriteria: Given/When/Then style is defined where useful
MSW and DB test isolation expectations are explicit
Live provider calls are opt-in and never required in CI
auditFocus: Tests that only assert implementation details

nodes.title: Orchestrator claim, plan, audit lifecycle CLI
nodes.summary: Implement the DAG orchestration lifecycle as tooling: claim nodes, create worktrees, ingest audit reports, manage P0/P1 repair loops, and draft P2/P3 follow-up nodes.
acceptanceCriteria: Two agents cannot claim the same node
P0/P1 audit findings keep the node in repair state
P2/P3 findings can draft new DAG nodes without hand-copying

nodes.title: Large-project synthetic scale harness
nodes.summary: Prove import, indexing, batch planning, queueing, and dashboard queries against generated large bridge bundles before real huge games force redesign.
acceptanceCriteria: A synthetic 1M+ character project imports and indexes within documented limits
Batch planning has a measured timing budget
Dashboard status queries have explicit performance expectations

nodes.title: Mutation and property-test quality gates
nodes.summary: Add mutation testing and property-style testing to the highest-risk TypeScript and Rust packages after the MVP gates are stable.
acceptanceCriteria: Selected schema/delta/protected-span logic has mutation or property coverage
Quality gates are meaningful and not prohibitively slow
Slow gates are scheduled intentionally

nodes.title: Harden exact upgrade recipe verification
nodes.summary: Harden the toolchain policy verifier so exact just upgrade command comparisons cannot silently accept ignored-error prefixes such as -corepack use pnpm@latest.
deliverables: Negative verifier fixture for a just upgrade command with a leading - prefix
Verifier diagnostics that name ignored-error prefixes when exact upgrade commands are checked
acceptanceCriteria: The verifier rejects or explicitly flags exact upgrade commands that use just ignored-error prefixes
A fixture containing -corepack use pnpm@latest fails verification instead of passing after prefix stripping
The current canonical just upgrade recipe remains accepted without ignored-error prefixes

nodes.title: Bridge schema v0.2 domain model
nodes.summary: Expand the bridge schema from fixture dialogue to game-localization primitives: assets, text surfaces, choices, UI, images-with-text, speaker metadata, source revisions, route context, and protected spans.
acceptanceCriteria: No raw string categories where enums are known
UUID7 ids are required and validated
Schema distinguishes parser-unknown from reader-unknown speaker state

nodes.title: Bridge contract validation
nodes.summary: Make TypeScript and Rust agree on bridge bundle JSON through shared fixtures and strict validation before patch, runtime, benchmark, and finding contracts are layered on.
acceptanceCriteria: Every committed bridge fixture validates in TypeScript and Rust
Invalid bridge fixtures fail with semantic errors
Bridge schema version behavior is documented

nodes.title: Multi-surface public bridge fixture corpus
nodes.summary: Replace the single-line fixture with public bridge fixtures covering dialogue, names, choices, UI labels, tutorial text, glossary terms, protected markup, and image-text metadata.
acceptanceCriteria: At least five text surfaces are represented
Protected spans include placeholders and inline control markup
Fixture supports 1->N locale branches
Patch exports and runtime reports are handled by later full-system artifact nodes

nodes.title: Patch and source revision semantics
nodes.summary: Define how source game versions, source text changes, patch exports, patch results, and delta patch packages relate across reruns and updated games.
acceptanceCriteria: A source typo patch can invalidate only affected units
Patch application reports incompatible source hashes clearly
Delta packages can be traced to a source revision

nodes.title: Runtime evidence schema v0.2
nodes.summary: Define trace, branch, capture, recording, approximation, and validation finding schemas for Utsushi-generated evidence.
acceptanceCriteria: Evidence can be linked back to bridge units
Approximation tiers are explicit
Screenshots and recordings are referenced, not embedded by default

nodes.title: Benchmark and cost report schema
nodes.summary: Define benchmark run, model/provider, cost, token, quality finding, deterministic QA, and human evaluation result schemas.
acceptanceCriteria: Reports capture exact model, provider, prompt preset, and timestamp
MTL baseline runs fit the same schema
QA agent evaluation is first-class

nodes.title: Event and finding taxonomy
nodes.summary: Define canonical event, task, finding, severity, provenance, and causality records for event-driven triage across the suite.
acceptanceCriteria: A QA finding can point to source annotation, style guide, model output, or patching cause
P0-P3 severities are distinct from localization quality categories
Events are append-only

nodes.title: Permission model and local-user baseline
nodes.summary: Introduce permission-based access primitives now, with a single all-permissions local user for MVP and a clean path to custom roles later.
acceptanceCriteria: No role strings are hardcoded into authorization checks
Every API mutation has a permission gate
MVP local mode remains frictionless

nodes.title: Localization quality taxonomy and benchmark protocol
nodes.summary: Choose and document the localization quality taxonomy and benchmark protocol, including MQM comparison, game-localization extensions, seeded defects, and QA-agent metrics.
acceptanceCriteria: Localization quality severities are distinct from DAG P0-P3
QA agents map findings to the taxonomy
Benchmark reports distinguish severity, category, root cause, and reviewer adjudication
auditFocus: Conflating orchestration severity with translation severity

nodes.title: Full contract validation
nodes.summary: Validate bridge, patch, delta, runtime, benchmark, asset-policy, and finding contracts together across TypeScript and Rust.
acceptanceCriteria: Every committed contract fixture validates in TypeScript and Rust
Invalid fixtures fail with semantic errors
Schema compatibility across the suite is documented
Permission and local-user contract fixtures validate with the rest of the suite

nodes.title: Asset localization policy schema
nodes.summary: Represent non-dialogue localization decisions for images with text, UI textures, audio/song metadata, fonts, credits, videos, and romanize/translate/do-not-translate choices.
acceptanceCriteria: Image text, song titles, UI art, fonts, credits, and videos have representable policy choices
Policies can be locale-branch scoped
Metadata-first support does not imply OCR or image editing is complete
auditFocus: Policy not tied to locale branches

nodes.title: Full-system golden artifact corpus
nodes.summary: Add expected patch exports, patch results, delta packages, runtime reports, benchmark reports, and findings for public fixtures after their schemas exist.
acceptanceCriteria: Full-system fixtures cover success and failure cases
Artifacts cite fixture hashes and schema versions
Fixtures are public-safe and reproducible

nodes.title: Kaifuu adapter trait and game profile model
nodes.summary: Define the Rust adapter abstraction for detection, extraction, patching, verification, asset listing, capability reporting, and game profiles.
acceptanceCriteria: Fixture engine uses the same trait as real engines
Adapters report patching limitations
Profile serialization is deterministic
auditFocus: Adapter methods too broad for review

nodes.title: Fixture engine on adapter architecture
nodes.summary: Refactor the existing hello-world fixture engine to prove the adapter trait before real engines are added.
deliverables: No behavior regression in hello world
acceptanceCriteria: Existing fixture commands still pass
Adapter capability output is visible
No duplicated fixture logic remains outside the adapter

nodes.title: Round-trip golden harness
nodes.summary: Build a deterministic harness for extract -> patch unchanged -> verify byte/output equivalence, plus translated patch application for public fixtures.
acceptanceCriteria: Unchanged extraction/patching is byte-identical where engine supports it
Translated patch failures cite exact unit and asset
Harness can run per adapter

nodes.title: Protected markup parser and span normalizer
nodes.summary: Parse engine scripting embedded in lines into protected spans so Itotori localizes text without breaking runtime commands.
acceptanceCriteria: Placeholders, ruby/furigana, color tags, name variables, and wait/control tags have coverage
Unknown tags are preserved safely
Clean text and raw text round-trip

nodes.title: .kaifuu delta package v0.2
nodes.summary: Promote the fixture whole-file delta into a real patch package format with metadata, source compatibility, changed-file entries, and efficient apply behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Delta patching is not deferred
Incompatible source hashes fail before writing output
Patch packages are deterministic and easy to distribute

nodes.title: Engine detection and profile CLI
nodes.summary: Add detect/profile commands that identify likely engines, emit editable game profiles, and report missing keys or platform constraints.
acceptanceCriteria: Detection output lists evidence, not LLM-style confidence
Profiles are stable TOML or JSON
Missing secrets/keys are represented without blocking unencrypted engines

nodes.title: RPG Maker MV/MZ adapter
nodes.summary: Implement extraction, patching, and verification for RPG Maker MV/MZ JSON assets across dialogue, choices, common events, database text, and UI-like strings.
acceptanceCriteria: Adapter extracts core map/event/database text
Patch writes preserve JSON structure and control codes
Unchanged round-trip is stable
Fixture source is documented, support boundary is explicit, and unsupported encrypted or compiled variants fail with semantic capability errors

nodes.title: Ren'Py adapter
nodes.summary: Implement a Ren'Py adapter for plaintext scripts and documented decompiled-script handoff, with clean extraction of dialogue, menus, labels, and translatable strings.
acceptanceCriteria: Plaintext .rpy fixtures round-trip
Menus and labels retain structure
Compiled .rpyc handling is explicitly profiled or delegated
Fixture source is documented, support boundary is explicit, and unsupported encrypted or compiled variants fail with semantic capability errors

nodes.title: KiriKiri/KAG plaintext adapter
nodes.summary: Implement KAG .ks extraction and patching for plaintext or already-extracted KiriKiri projects, including speaker/name context and inline command preservation.
acceptanceCriteria: Dialogue, names, choices, and command-heavy lines are covered
Reader-unknown speaker state can be represented
Round-trip keeps commands stable
Fixture source is documented, support boundary is explicit, and unsupported encrypted or compiled variants fail with semantic capability errors

nodes.title: Patch result and verification v0.2
nodes.summary: Make Kaifuu patching emit structured success/failure reports suitable for Itotori ingestion, dashboarding, and triage.
acceptanceCriteria: Failures cite asset, unit, adapter, and cause
Partial patch attempts are visible
Successful reports include output hashes

nodes.title: Binary patcher core
nodes.summary: Build shared binary offset rewriting utilities for engines that store text in string tables or bytecode with referenced offsets.
acceptanceCriteria: String growth/shrink rewrites offsets correctly
Shift-JIS and UTF-16LE cases have fixtures
Failures avoid corrupt writes

nodes.title: Wolf RPG Editor adapter
nodes.summary: Implement a Wolf RPG Editor adapter once the binary patching utilities are ready.
acceptanceCriteria: Core text tables extract and patch
Binary layout changes are verified
Unsupported encrypted variants are profiled

nodes.title: BGI/Ethornell adapter
nodes.summary: Implement BGI/Ethornell bytecode string extraction and patching with offset rewriting and Shift-JIS handling.
acceptanceCriteria: Known header and no-header variants are profiled
False-positive string references are tested
Patch output remains loadable in fixtures

nodes.title: Encryption and key profile boundary
nodes.summary: Define how adapters request keys, compression options, and platform-assisted discovery without coupling extraction/patching to Windows-only discovery code.
acceptanceCriteria: Profiles can reference required keys without storing secrets accidentally
Linux/macOS extraction remains possible when keys are provided
Discovery and patching remain separate

nodes.title: SiglusEngine research slice
nodes.summary: Produce a concrete implementation plan for Siglus extraction/patching from reference implementations, including scene VM opportunities and key discovery boundaries.
acceptanceCriteria: Scene.pck/Gameexe.dat requirements are clear
Key discovery is separated from pure parsing
VM value for validation is assessed

verification.value: Parser spike tests if safe fixtures exist

nodes.title: TyranoScript adapter
nodes.summary: Implement TyranoScript extraction and patching by reusing KAG-like markup parsing where appropriate.
acceptanceCriteria: Dialogue, choices, and common tags extract
Unsupported plugins are preserved or reported
Patch round-trip is stable

nodes.title: Majiro and CatSystem2 research adapters
nodes.summary: Research and spike Majiro and CatSystem2 support enough to split each into implementation specs with real fixtures.
acceptanceCriteria: Each engine has a scoped next implementation node or explicit blocker
Reference implementations are cataloged
Binary risks are documented

nodes.title: Unity localization asset research
nodes.summary: Assess Unity text extraction and patching strategies for I2 Localization, TextMeshPro, Addressables, and common asset bundle layouts.
acceptanceCriteria: Common Unity localization storage patterns are separated
Patch feasibility is rated by asset type
Runtime validation implications are documented

nodes.title: Encoding, normalization, and patch safety policy
nodes.summary: Define encoding, Unicode normalization, newline, atomic-write, temp-dir, path traversal, rollback, and partial-write safety rules for patching.
acceptanceCriteria: Shift-JIS, UTF-8, and UTF-16LE handling is specified
Patch writers use atomic output rules where feasible
Path traversal and partial writes fail safely

nodes.title: Asset inventory and non-text surface extraction
nodes.summary: Emit asset manifests for images with text, UI textures, song/audio metadata, fonts, credits, and video assets without promising full editing support.
acceptanceCriteria: Adapters can report non-text surfaces even when they cannot patch them
Asset manifests are engine-agnostic
No OCR or image editing capability is implied unless explicitly implemented

nodes.title: Siglus pure extraction and patching adapter
nodes.summary: Implement pure Siglus extraction and patching once research, key-profile boundaries, and binary patching utilities are ready.
acceptanceCriteria: Pure parsing/patching is separated from key discovery
Unsupported encrypted cases fail with key-requirement errors
Patch output is verified against fixtures

nodes.title: Majiro adapter
nodes.summary: Implement Majiro extraction and patching after the research spike splits out concrete format requirements.
acceptanceCriteria: String reference discovery is fixture-tested
Unsupported bytecode variants fail semantically
Patch safety follows the shared binary patcher policy

nodes.title: CatSystem2 adapter
nodes.summary: Implement CatSystem2 extraction and patching after the research spike identifies safe fixtures and binary string table requirements.
acceptanceCriteria: Shift-JIS string table handling is tested
Unsupported format variants are reported clearly
Binary patching is safe on failure

nodes.title: Unity text asset adapter prototype
nodes.summary: Prototype Unity localization asset extraction for one selected pattern after research separates I2, TextMeshPro, Addressables, and asset bundles.
acceptanceCriteria: Prototype names the Unity localization pattern it supports
Unsupported asset bundle mutation is explicit
Runtime validation implications are documented

nodes.title: Asset OCR and text-region extraction prototype
nodes.summary: Prototype OCR and text-region extraction for image/UI assets as evidence and drafting input without silently treating OCR as truth.
acceptanceCriteria: Fixture images produce text regions with provenance
Uncertain OCR is represented as findings
OCR output does not overwrite source asset policy decisions

nodes.title: Engine adapter development playbook
nodes.summary: Create the repeatable workflow for adding engines with parallel coding agents: fixture requirements, parser spikes, round-trip tests, error codes, and remote Windows helper patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: A new agent can start an engine adapter without hidden context
Remote Windows execution is documented as an optional helper
Every adapter PR has a fixture and round-trip test checklist

verification.value: Apply playbook to one adapter issue

nodes.title: Itotori persistence schema v0.2
nodes.summary: Replace hello-world persistence with real project, source bundle, unit, asset, source revision, locale branch, event, finding, user, permission, and artifact tables.
acceptanceCriteria: Schema supports 1->N locale branches
Events are append-only
Permission primitives exist from day one
No dashboard reads from local JSON

nodes.title: Repository and service layer split
nodes.summary: Separate DB repositories, domain services, CLI handlers, and dashboard API handlers so future agents do not add ad hoc SQL or UI-coupled state.
acceptanceCriteria: CLI and API share services
No direct SQL in app UI handlers
Tests cover repository and service boundaries

nodes.title: Event outbox and job queue foundation
nodes.summary: Add durable event outbox and job queue primitives for agent tasks, deterministic tools, reruns, and triage loops.
acceptanceCriteria: Jobs are idempotent or explicitly non-idempotent
Retries preserve error history
Events can trigger follow-up jobs without synchronous coupling

nodes.title: Typed API layer and local dashboard backend
nodes.summary: Replace the hello-world API with typed project, import, branch, findings, decisions, benchmark, and runtime evidence endpoints.
acceptanceCriteria: Every mutation checks permissions
Response schemas are typed and tested
MSW handlers mirror real API shapes
MSW handlers are generated from or checked against real API response schemas

nodes.title: Dashboard application shell
nodes.summary: Build the non-marketing workbench shell for projects, locale branches, style guide, glossary, jobs, QA findings, decisions, runtime evidence, and benchmarks.
acceptanceCriteria: A non-technical reviewer can find pending decisions
Dashboard reads only through APIs
Loading, empty, and error states exist
MSW handlers are generated from or checked against real API response schemas

nodes.title: Bridge import workflow
nodes.summary: Import Kaifuu bridge bundles into projects, preserve source revisions, index units/assets, and expose import status in CLI and dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: Invalid bridge bundles fail before DB writes
Reimported source revisions are diffed, not blindly duplicated
Import metrics are visible

nodes.title: Conversational style guide builder
nodes.summary: Build the human-in-the-loop style guide creation workflow inspired by product-brief creation, with explicit decisions and versioned policy output.
acceptanceCriteria: Human decisions are structured and versioned
Style guide changes can trigger affected reruns
The UX presents context instead of isolated random lines

nodes.title: Glossary and terminology index
nodes.summary: Implement terminology storage, preferred translations, aliases, source references, semantic search hooks, and conflict handling.
acceptanceCriteria: Terms are searchable by exact and semantic-like fields
Preferred translations are locale-branch scoped
Conflicts become findings or decisions

nodes.title: LLM provider abstraction
nodes.summary: Create provider-neutral model invocation interfaces with an OpenRouter implementation first and local OpenAI-compatible endpoints later.
acceptanceCriteria: Itotori core does not depend directly on OpenRouter response quirks
Provider/model capabilities are explicit
Free/logging provider policy is represented
No live credential is required for CI; live runs are opt-in and recorded as non-committed artifacts

verification.value: No live API required in CI

nodes.title: Model registry, presets, and cost ledger
nodes.summary: Track model/provider identity, prompt presets, router settings, token estimates, actual usage, and per-job cost.
acceptanceCriteria: Every LLM job records model/provider/preset
Cost reports separate estimated and billed usage
Provider fallback decisions are auditable
No live credential is required for CI; live runs are opt-in and recorded as non-committed artifacts

nodes.title: Agent and deterministic tool registry
nodes.summary: Separate LLM-powered agents from deterministic tools, with typed inputs, outputs, provenance, and event emission for both.
acceptanceCriteria: Deterministic tools never masquerade as agents
Agent outputs require rationales/findings when scoring
Tool outputs are reproducible

nodes.title: Context artifact framework
nodes.summary: Store, version, index, and retrieve context artifacts such as scene summaries, character notes, route maps, speaker labels, and terminology candidates.
acceptanceCriteria: Artifacts cite source units and producing agent/tool
Source changes invalidate affected artifacts
Agents retrieve context through tools, not opaque legacy RAG

nodes.title: Scene summary agent
nodes.summary: Implement a focused scene-summary agent that produces source-language summaries with source-unit citations and rerun invalidation.
acceptanceCriteria: Summaries cite covered units
No character relationship or route mapping scope creep
Outputs are versioned by source revision and style policy when relevant

nodes.title: Character relationship agent
nodes.summary: Implement a focused character bio and relationship artifact agent with cited evidence and change tracking.
acceptanceCriteria: Character claims cite scenes or lines
Relationship changes over time can be represented
Unknown or ambiguous identity is explicit

nodes.title: Route and choice map agent
nodes.summary: Build route and choice map artifacts from bridge structure and runtime evidence, enabling scalable localization of branching games.
acceptanceCriteria: Static bridge edges and runtime branch observations can coexist
Large route maps are chunked
Choice text links to source units

nodes.title: Terminology candidate agent
nodes.summary: Suggest glossary candidates from source text with citations, frequency, context, and proposed handling without auto-escalating every term to humans.
acceptanceCriteria: Candidates include evidence and frequency
Human escalation is reserved for material conflicts
Accepted terms become locale-scoped glossary entries

nodes.title: Speaker and hidden-identity labeling agent
nodes.summary: Label speakers and hidden identities while preserving the difference between unknown-to-parser and unknown-to-reader.
acceptanceCriteria: Labels never overwrite source display names
Reader-hidden identities are protected from accidental translation spoilers
Ambiguity is represented explicitly

nodes.title: Batch planner and context packer
nodes.summary: Plan translation and QA batches from units, scene boundaries, route context, glossary hits, and model context limits.
acceptanceCriteria: Works for tiny games and million-character projects
Batches include cited context instead of whole-game prompts
Token estimates are stored

nodes.title: Translation drafting engine
nodes.summary: Generate locale-branch drafts from batches, style guide, glossary, context artifacts, protected spans, and model routing.
acceptanceCriteria: Protected spans are preserved or findings are emitted
Drafts are tied to source revision and style guide version
Cost and model metadata are recorded per batch
No live credential is required for CI; live runs are opt-in and recorded as non-committed artifacts

nodes.title: Deterministic QA tool suite
nodes.summary: Implement deterministic checks for protected spans, empty translations, charset, line length, punctuation policy, glossary exact matches, and patch export integrity.
acceptanceCriteria: Every deterministic failure includes exact unit and repair hint
Tools can run without LLMs
False positives are fixture-tested

nodes.title: LLM QA agents and scored findings
nodes.summary: Implement focused LLM QA agents for style adherence, semantic drift, tone/register, and unresolved terminology, using finding-backed scores rather than confidence.
acceptanceCriteria: Scores require findings that explain the gap
A second judge can regrade findings
Each agent owns a narrow issue class
No live credential is required for CI; live runs are opt-in and recorded as non-committed artifacts
auditFocus: QA findings worse than no QA

nodes.title: Finding triage and root-cause router
nodes.summary: Route QA and human findings to likely causes: translator mistake, stale context, source annotation issue, glossary conflict, style guide issue, Kaifuu patching, or runtime evidence.
acceptanceCriteria: Triage never assumes translator fault by default
Root-cause hypotheses cite evidence
P0/P1 findings can trigger repair jobs

nodes.title: Human decision queue UX
nodes.summary: Build the polished queue for style disputes, important glossary conflicts, draft feedback, screenshots, comparisons, reasoning, batch actions, and rerun triggers.
acceptanceCriteria: A reviewer sees source, draft, context, screenshot/evidence, and agent reasoning together
Batch approvals/rejections are possible
Low-value issues can be deferred without losing traceability
MSW handlers are generated from or checked against real API response schemas

nodes.title: Playtest feedback intake
nodes.summary: Ingest human feedback from playable or runtime-reviewed drafts into the same triage queue, starting with manual/imported playtest reports before external community integrations.
acceptanceCriteria: Feedback can include screenshots, save/context, and line references
Style preference disputes are labeled distinctly
Duplicate reports aggregate evidence
Feedback enters the same decision and triage workflows as QA findings

nodes.title: Patch export and Kaifuu handoff v0.2
nodes.summary: Export patch-ready translations with source compatibility metadata, protected-span mappings, and deterministic preflight checks before Kaifuu writes files.
acceptanceCriteria: Exports fail if deterministic P0 checks fail
Export includes source revision compatibility
Kaifuu receives engine-agnostic data only

nodes.title: Benchmark harness and MTL baseline
nodes.summary: Run raw MTL baselines, deterministic QA, seeded defect tests, QA-agent evaluation, cost reports, and quality summaries through one benchmark harness.
acceptanceCriteria: Raw MTL is a comparable baseline
QA agents are benchmarked separately from final drafts
Reports are reproducible with fixture hashes
Reports include raw MTL, Itotori draft, QA-agent evaluation, deterministic QA output, token/cost data, and fixture hashes

nodes.title: Cost and quality dashboard
nodes.summary: Expose benchmark, cost, token, model, provider, QA, and draft-quality trends in the dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: The $25 indie localization target can be tracked empirically
Provider/model changes are visible
QA false positives/negatives are represented
MSW handlers are generated from or checked against real API response schemas

nodes.title: End-to-end draft iteration loop
nodes.summary: Wire import, context, draft, QA, decision, patch export, Kaifuu patch, Utsushi validation, feedback, triage, and rerun into one vertical iteration loop.
acceptanceCriteria: A P1 QA finding can trigger repair and re-audit
Human decisions update policy/glossary/draft state and rerun affected work
The loop is event-driven, not a brittle linear script

verification.value: End-to-end v0.2 loop command introduced by this node

nodes.title: Locale branch foundation
nodes.summary: Make 1->N localization first-class before drafting: independent target branches, branch-scoped policies, glossary, drafts, QA, exports, and benchmark costs.
acceptanceCriteria: No JP->EN hardcoding remains
Multiple target locales can coexist for one source revision
Branch-specific glossary and style decisions do not leak

nodes.title: Search and indexing infrastructure decision
nodes.summary: Decide exact and semantic indexing strategy for glossary terms, scenes, context artifacts, feedback, and agent tools, including pgvector handling.
acceptanceCriteria: Exact search indexes are specified
Semantic search tool interface is defined
Docker and CI behavior is clear if pgvector is unavailable

nodes.title: Provider credentials, routing, and recording policy
nodes.summary: Define provider boundaries, OpenRouter quirks, local OpenAI-compatible endpoints, secret storage, prompt logging, free-provider policy, structured-output fallback, and recorded fixtures.
acceptanceCriteria: No live credential is required for CI
Live provider runs are opt-in and recorded as non-committed artifacts
Free/logging provider policy is explicit

nodes.title: Seeded localization defect corpus
nodes.summary: Create fixtures with known localization defects for deterministic QA, LLM QA, triage, benchmarks, and false-positive calibration.
acceptanceCriteria: Lost markup, wrong speaker, style violation, glossary drift, semantic mistranslation, over-literal MTL, line length/UI overflow, and false-positive QA cases are represented
Expected findings map to the quality taxonomy
Fixture hashes are recorded

nodes.title: Feedback source model and manual import stub
nodes.summary: Define feedback sources, manual report entry, screenshot/save/context attachment shapes, dedupe keys, and style-dispute labels before external integrations.
acceptanceCriteria: Manual feedback can include screenshot, save/context, line reference, and reporter note
Style preference disputes are distinct from objective defects
Duplicates can aggregate evidence

nodes.title: Translation memory and reuse index
nodes.summary: Store reusable source/target segments, fuzzy matches, reuse provenance, locale-branch scope, and draft prefill hooks to reduce cost and improve consistency.
acceptanceCriteria: Repeated lines and near-duplicates can reuse prior decisions
Memory is locale-branch scoped
Reuse is costed and auditable rather than invisible

nodes.title: Asset localization decision workflow
nodes.summary: Let humans set locale-branch policy for images with text, song titles, UI art, fonts, videos, romanization, full localization, or do-not-translate handling.
acceptanceCriteria: Asset localization choices are explicit and locale-scoped
Metadata-only assets can be tracked without patch support
Human decisions are respected by exports and QA

nodes.title: Local model provider parity
nodes.summary: Prove LM Studio or another OpenAI-compatible local endpoint can run drafting and QA loops with explicit capability gaps and zero/estimated cost accounting.
acceptanceCriteria: Local endpoint can draft and QA fixture batches
Capability gaps are visible
Costs are recorded as zero or estimated without corrupting OpenRouter reports

nodes.title: External community channel integrations
nodes.summary: Integrate GitHub issues, forms, Discord/forum exports, or other community channels after the manual feedback model is proven.
acceptanceCriteria: Imported reports keep source/channel metadata
Duplicates aggregate evidence
Privacy-sensitive data is redacted or flagged

nodes.title: Repair and rerun orchestration skeleton
nodes.summary: Implement the minimal event-driven repair loop that turns P0/P1 findings and human decisions into targeted rerun jobs before the full E2E iteration milestone.
acceptanceCriteria: P0/P1 findings can trigger targeted repair jobs
Human decisions rerun affected work without rerunning the whole project unless necessary
Repair history remains auditable

nodes.title: Model and prompt experiment runner
nodes.summary: Run reproducible model, provider, prompt, preset, router, and tool-strategy experiments so agent defaults are benchmarked rather than assumed.
acceptanceCriteria: Reports compare cost, latency, quality taxonomy findings, structured-output reliability, and provider failure rate
Selected presets are reproducible and auditable
Live calls remain opt-in and recorded as non-committed artifacts
auditFocus: Provider failure rates hidden from reports

nodes.title: Localization workspace UX
nodes.summary: Build the main reviewer workspace for browsing projects, scenes, units, assets, source/draft/final comparisons, searchable context, manual corrections, and edit history.
acceptanceCriteria: A reviewer who does not speak the source language can navigate by translated summaries and context
Manual edits enter the same event and repair loop
Dashboard reads through APIs rather than direct DB access
auditFocus: Queue-only UX that hides project context

nodes.title: Asset localization drafting and QA loop
nodes.summary: Extend drafting, review, QA, and export workflows to image/UI text and other asset-localization payloads once OCR and asset inventory exist.
acceptanceCriteria: Image/UI text can be drafted, reviewed, QA’d, and exported as decisions or patch payloads
Unsupported engine asset patching remains explicit
Asset localization does not pretend every asset is editable
auditFocus: Text workflow assumptions leaking into assets

nodes.title: Style dispute adjudication workflow
nodes.summary: Add explicit accept, reject, defer, and style-guide-amendment handling for controversial localization feedback.
acceptanceCriteria: Style feedback can be accepted, rejected, deferred, or converted into a style-guide amendment
Rejected feedback keeps rationale and does not reappear as unresolved QA
Accepted style changes trigger affected reruns

nodes.title: Utsushi runtime adapter trait
nodes.summary: Define runtime adapter APIs for trace, branch discovery, capture, smoke validation, capability reporting, and approximation tiers.
acceptanceCriteria: Fixture runtime uses the same trait as future adapters
Adapters can be useful without pixel-perfect emulation
Approximation limits are explicit

nodes.title: Fixture runtime on adapter architecture
nodes.summary: Refactor the existing fixture trace/capture/smoke code onto the runtime adapter trait with no hello-world regression.
acceptanceCriteria: Current Utsushi commands still pass
Fixture reports fidelity tier explicitly
No special-case CLI logic remains

nodes.title: Runtime evidence ingestion
nodes.summary: Persist runtime evidence in Itotori with links to projects, source revisions, locale branches, bridge units, artifacts, and validation findings.
acceptanceCriteria: Evidence links back to bridge units
Multiple runtime runs can coexist
Artifact paths are portable and not raw blobs by default

nodes.title: Screenshot and artifact store
nodes.summary: Provide local artifact storage conventions for screenshots, frame captures, traces, and recordings, with references stored in Postgres.
acceptanceCriteria: Artifacts are reproducibly named
DB rows reference artifacts without embedding large files
Cleanup cannot delete source game files

nodes.title: Runtime review dashboard
nodes.summary: Upgrade the runtime dashboard from hello-world status to reviewable traces, screenshots, branch points, validation findings, and comparison views.
acceptanceCriteria: A reviewer can inspect where a line appeared
Screenshots and traces link to source units and drafts
Approximation/fidelity limits are visible
MSW handlers are generated from or checked against real API response schemas

nodes.title: RPG Maker MV/MZ validation probe
nodes.summary: Implement a pragmatic validation probe for RPG Maker MV/MZ projects: route/text trace extraction, lightweight launch or static validation, and screenshot hooks where feasible.
acceptanceCriteria: Adapter produces useful validation evidence without requiring pixel-perfect porting
Limitations are explicit
It supports the first real-engine MVP loop
Evidence tier is visible wherever evidence is shown

nodes.title: Ren'Py route and text probe
nodes.summary: Produce runtime-like validation evidence for Ren'Py projects through script structure, route/menu analysis, and optional launch/capture hooks.
acceptanceCriteria: Menus and labels are traced
Evidence links to bridge units
Unsupported runtime capture is represented honestly
Evidence tier is visible wherever evidence is shown

nodes.title: KiriKiri/KAG trace probe
nodes.summary: Build a trace-focused KAG validation probe for command-heavy visual novel scripts, emphasizing text order, speaker context, and branch points.
acceptanceCriteria: Trace order is useful for review
Speaker and branch metadata link back to bridge units
Unsupported rendering is clearly tiered
Evidence tier is visible wherever evidence is shown

nodes.title: Branch point explorer
nodes.summary: Use runtime probes and route maps to expose branch points, reachable text, screenshots, and validation gaps.
acceptanceCriteria: Reviewers can see branch coverage
Unvisited text can be identified
Large route maps remain navigable

nodes.title: Playable review package prototype
nodes.summary: Prototype a local playable review slice with annotations and evidence capture for at least one supported engine.
acceptanceCriteria: A reviewer can play a small patched slice and submit contextual feedback
Feedback links to runtime evidence
The prototype does not block MVP if fidelity is limited

nodes.title: Agent validation hooks over runtime evidence
nodes.summary: Let QA agents inspect runtime evidence through tools for layout, missing text, wrong branch, screenshot OCR hints, and mismatch findings.
acceptanceCriteria: Agents request evidence through tools
Findings cite screenshots/traces
Deterministic runtime checks run before LLM review
Runtime evidence can trigger findings for visible overflow, missing text, broken markup artifacts, wrong scene or branch, and screenshot/evidence mismatch
Every runtime-evidence QA finding records evidence tier

nodes.title: Scene VM feasibility report
nodes.summary: Evaluate when engine-specific VMs are worth building for validation, branch navigation, screenshots, recordings, and browser playback.
acceptanceCriteria: Accuracy target is validation usefulness first, pixel-perfect second
Siglus, RPG Maker, and KiriKiri are compared
Build-vs-skip criteria are explicit
The report can create follow-up DAG nodes without blocking MVP runtime probes

nodes.title: Browser playback experiment
nodes.summary: Experiment with WebAssembly/browser playback or partial runtime visualization for one selected engine family.
acceptanceCriteria: Prototype shows whether browser playback helps review
Limitations are measured
No MVP dependency is introduced

nodes.title: Cross-platform launch and capture harness
nodes.summary: Build a pragmatic launch/capture harness for native, Wine/Proton, or browser-backed probes with process control, screenshots, timeouts, and semantic errors.
acceptanceCriteria: Launch failures produce semantic evidence rather than hanging
Wine/Proton/native assumptions are explicit
Screenshots are stored through the artifact store

nodes.title: First real-engine end-to-end vertical slice
nodes.summary: Run the full Itotori/Kaifuu/Utsushi loop on a public or private-local RPG Maker MV/MZ fixture without relying on the synthetic fixture engine.
acceptanceCriteria: Extract, draft, QA, patch, delta apply, runtime evidence, feedback, and rerun all work
No raw copyrighted assets are committed
All failures are structured findings

nodes.title: Playable draft feedback loop
nodes.summary: Make human draft feedback from a playable or runtime-reviewed slice flow into triage, decisions, repair jobs, and updated patch output.
acceptanceCriteria: A typo correction can be submitted with context and batched
Style disputes can become decision queue items
Repair jobs update only affected work where possible

nodes.title: Cost and quality target report
nodes.summary: Produce the first credible MVP report comparing raw MTL, Itotori drafts, deterministic QA, LLM QA, cost, and runtime evidence on fixture and private-local data.
acceptanceCriteria: Reports avoid unverifiable public claims
MTL baseline is included
The $25 target is evaluated with real token/count data
Reports include raw MTL, Itotori draft, QA-agent evaluation, deterministic QA output, token/cost data, and fixture hashes

nodes.title: Multi-engine MVP demonstration
nodes.summary: Demonstrate that the architecture is engine-agnostic by running supported slices across RPG Maker, Ren'Py, and KAG-style fixtures.
acceptanceCriteria: At least three engine families have extraction/patching paths
Runtime evidence fidelity is clearly tiered
Itotori code remains engine-agnostic

nodes.title: MVP release hardening
nodes.summary: Prepare the MVP for public use: docs, install flow, security posture, known limitations, examples, green required checks, and roadmap continuation.
acceptanceCriteria: A new user can run public fixture demos
Known legal/safety boundaries are practical and not overstated
CI and hello-world workflows are required and green
